perturbation.original_text: Section 13.9 of the Development Agreement is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Notwithstanding the above, Bioamber may fund the BRI Project up until the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrates the ability to produce succinic acid (or salts thereof) from any feedstock at a concentration of [***] grams/liter succinic acid (or salts thereof). Within thirty (30) days of the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrating such production levels of succinic acid, Bioamber will cease any further funding and/or other support for the BRI Project. Further Bioamber will require that any unexpended funds received from Bioamber be utilized for a project other than the BRI Project.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 13.9 of the Development Agreement is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Notwithstanding the above, Bioamber may fund the BRI Project indefinitely, regardless of whether the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrates the ability to produce succinic acid (or salts thereof) from any feedstock at a concentration of [***] grams/liter succinic acid (or salts thereof). Bioamber may continue funding and/or other support for the BRI Project at its discretion. Further Bioamber may decide how any unexpended funds received from Bioamber will be utilized.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a condition upon which Bioamber's funding of the BRI project must cease. The modified text removes this condition and grants Bioamber indefinite funding and discretionary use of unexpended funds, creating a contradiction within the agreement itself and potentially affecting the obligations of both parties. This introduces uncertainty in contract enforcement.
perturbation.law_explanation: By removing the specific condition for funding cessation, the modified text contradicts the principle of mutual agreement and creates uncertainty regarding the enforceable terms of the contract. The Restatement (Second) of Contracts emphasizes the importance of clearly defined obligations, and the modification undermines this by making a key term indefinite.
perturbation.location: Amendment To the Development Agreement, Section A
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a condition for the cessation of funding, while the changed text removes this condition, allowing indefinite funding. The Law Explanation states this "contradicts the principle of mutual agreement and creates uncertainty regarding the enforceable terms of the contract," and that "the modification undermines this by making a key term indefinite." As the definition of Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction states, this type of contradiction "arises when contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions, making the contract unenforceable." By removing the defined condition for the end of funding, the modified contract deviates from its original, enforceable definition, and creates an unenforceable obligation.

perturbation.original_text: Bioamber shall use best efforts to obtain regulatory approvals for the use of the CB1 Strain in all countries where Bioamber and/or a Bioamber licensee are using any strain other than the CB1 strain for the production of succinic acid and/or salts thereof. Additionally, Bioamber shall use best efforts to scale up the CB1 Strain and fermentation protocols utilizing the CB1 Strain.
perturbation.changed_text: Bioamber may, at its own convenience, seek regulatory approvals for the use of the CB1 Strain, and is not required to pursue such approvals diligently. Bioamber is encouraged to scale up the CB1 Strain but has no binding duty to do so.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses the phrase "best efforts," which has a specific legal meaning that implies a high standard of diligence. The modified text changes "best efforts" to discretionary actions, effectively lowering the standard of performance required of Bioamber. This contradiction impacts the enforceability of the clause and the reliance Cargill can place on Bioamber's actions. Also, it has been said that 'best efforts' is often interpreted as 'reasonable efforts' which may be different from the actual understanding.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Implied Duty of Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: The original "best efforts" clause implies a duty of good faith and fair dealing in seeking regulatory approvals. The modified text, which allows Bioamber to act at its own convenience and without diligence, undermines this duty. UCC § 1-304 imposes an obligation of good faith in the performance of every contract, and the modification weakens this obligation.
perturbation.location: Amendment To the Commercial License, Section A
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes

perturbation.justification: The original text's 'best efforts' clause creates a strong obligation, while the changed text allows Bioamber to act 'at its own convenience' and without diligence, which contradicts the implied duty of good faith as described in the law explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1, UCC § 1-304, states that 'Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement.' The change fundamentally weakens this obligation by removing the requirement of diligent effort, resulting in a clear contradiction. The modified clause diminishes Bioamber's responsibility, deviating from standard legal expectations and potentially rendering the clause unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: In order to enable such conversion, Bioamber will put in place agreements with the owners/operators of the Sarnia Plant that will enable Bioamber to require such conversion of the Sarnia Plant to solely use CB1 for the manufacture of succinic acid as described above.
perturbation.changed_text: Bioamber will attempt to have informal discussions with the owners/operators of the Sarnia Plant regarding a possible conversion, but is under no obligation to secure binding agreements for such conversion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Bioamber 'will put in place agreements' that 'will enable Bioamber to require such conversion', indicating a firm commitment and a legally enforceable obligation. The modified text weakens this obligation significantly by replacing it with 'attempt to have informal discussions' and removing any binding requirement. This introduces significant uncertainty about the actual obligation of Bioamber and alters the nature of the contractual agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: By changing a firm commitment to a non-binding attempt, the modified text fundamentally alters the obligation of performance under the contract. Section 228 of the Restatement (Second) of Contracts addresses contract interpretation, and it emphasizes the importance of clear and definite obligations. This change creates ambiguity and potentially renders the clause unenforceable.
perturbation.location: Amendment To the Development Agreement, Section B
perturbation.justification: The original text imposes a firm obligation on Bioamber to secure agreements for the conversion of the Sarnia Plant. The changed text weakens this obligation to merely attempting informal discussions without any binding commitment. The Law Explanation references Section 228 of the Restatement (Second) of Contracts, which emphasizes the importance of clear and definite obligations. Although there are no law snippets, the shift from a firm commitment to a non-binding discussion creates ambiguity and potentially renders the clause unenforceable, which aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology leading to a Legal Contradiction, where contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions. Therefore, the change creates a significant contradiction.
